questions.question: When is the K-Means algorithm said to have 'Converged'?

answers.a: When K reaches the maximum value
answers.b: When the centroids stop moving between iterations
answers.c: When all points belong to a single cluster

explanations.a: Convergence is about stability of the clusters, not the value of K.
explanations.b: Convergence happens when no data points switch clusters and centroids remain in the same position.
explanations.c: This would only happen if K=1, which is not the definition of convergence.
explanations.d: The algorithm usually requires multiple iterations to find the best centroids.

questions.question: What does WCSS (Within-Cluster Sum of Squares) measure?

answers.c: How compact or 'tight' the clusters are
answers.d: The time taken for the algorithm to converge

explanations.a: WCSS measures internal cluster distance, not inter-cluster distance.
explanations.b: WCSS is a mathematical score, not a count.
explanations.c: A lower WCSS means data points are huddled closely around their centroids.
explanations.d: WCSS is a spatial metric measuring quality, not execution time.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the 'Elbow Method'?

answers.b: To help guess the optimal number for K

explanations.a: The Elbow Method uses WCSS, not Silhouette Score.
explanations.b: The 'elbow' point on the WCSS-vs-K graph indicates the point where adding more clusters provides diminishing returns.
explanations.c: It uses a line graph of WCSS, not a scatter plot of the data.
explanations.d: Standardization is a prerequisite for clustering, not the goal of the Elbow Method.

questions.question: What does a Silhouette Score near 1 indicate?

answers.b: The algorithm failed to reach convergence
answers.c: Excellent clustering where clusters are tight and well-separated
answers.d: The dataset contains too many features

explanations.a: A score near 0 indicates overlapping clusters.
explanations.b: Silhouette score measures quality, not convergence.
explanations.c: A score near 1 means clusters are distinct and points are very similar to their own cluster.
explanations.d: Silhouette score measures cluster quality, not feature count.

questions.question: In the K-Means process, what happens during the 'Centroid Update' step?

answers.a: A new centroid is calculated as the average position of points in a group
answers.b: Points are assigned to the nearest centroid
answers.c: The value of K is increased by one

explanations.a: The new centroid is found by calculating the mean (average) of all points currently assigned to that group.
explanations.c: The value of K remains constant throughout the algorithm's execution.
explanations.d: Reshuffling is not part of the centroid update process.
